pattern: Compute one root's total distance with a post-order pass, then reroute the answer to every neighbor in a second pass: crossing an edge subtracts one for each node in the subtree you leave and adds one for everyone else.
hints: Solving it independently for each node is too slow; the answers for adjacent nodes differ by a simple, predictable amount.
First gather subtree sizes and the distance sum for a single chosen root. Then think about what changes when you move the root to a child across one edge.
Root the tree at 0. Post-order to get count[v] (subtree size) and dist[0] = sum of depths. Pre-order to propagate: dist[child] = dist[parent] - count[child] + (n - count[child]). Return dist.

edge_inputs.description: single node has zero total distance
two nodes are each distance one from the other
a path graph stresses the linear reroute
a star puts every leaf at distance two from each other through the center
edges listed with the larger label first

stress.description: large random tree built by attaching each new node to a uniformly random earlier node
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    edges = []
    for v in range(1, n):
        p = rng.randrange(0, v)
        edges.append([v, p])
    rng.shuffle(edges)
    return [n, edges]
stress.note: guaranteed connected tree on n nodes

stress.description: long path graph, the worst case for the reroute arithmetic
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    edges = [[i, i + 1] for i in range(n - 1)]
    rng.shuffle(edges)
    return [n, edges]
